item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm with everyone else disappointed in no easter eggs, but this is otherwise a solid little article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was under the impression that 2851 refers to the area affected

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is bad crit. Saying "Redactions would add more" is entirely nonsensical.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: For reference, the source for the image used in this article is here and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I highlighted the whole page, hoping for a hidden Easter egg (since the text would no longer be red) so that was a slight disappointment, feels like a missed opportunity. But other than that, I enjoyed the article. Simple, straightforward, avoids the trope of yet another deity that forcibly bends people to their will. +1 From me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this, but I feel that it could have been improved if it followed the "Arson, Murder and Jaywalking" trope, where one of the things 2851-1 compels is bizarre or weird.

Anyway, this is relevant: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U0yPj8oOR_k

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
2851-1 is a new contender for favorite character on the wiki. For everyone who wants an audio version of the Herald of Taros'zedbreet (whom I am now calling Harold), you write it as a Tale and I'll voice it. I love that it's an invisible evil Navi. "Hey! Listen! Steal that car! Press Z to target Carl from IT's weak point!"

Anyway, here's an audio adaptation:

https://youtu.be/obh0s2sTjK8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could play around with the "Oops, I accidentally highlighted the text." idea. I know this article has grown up now and is unlikely to undergo much editing but in the event of a re-write for whatever reason it would be awesome to have some sort of hidden audio file or otherwise which plays when the text is highlighted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wing 9 would give me a head ache.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting but I don't like the word phenomenon. It makes it seem like it is something in their head and not something that actually exists.

Might wanna look up the definition of "phenomenon"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That isn't what "phenomenon" means. Phenomena generally have to exist because they are by definition things that occur. Rain is a phenomenon. Caribou migration is a phenomenon. Comets are phenomena.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting but I don't like the word phenomenon. It makes it seem like it is something in their head and not something that actually exists. I do like it though. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This impressive article is better than anything I can write. It really has me…

seeing red.

*gunshots, explosions*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. But there is a lot of potential for more. When did this first occur? What happened when it did first occur? Perhaps include some reports of testing with D-class? You could perhaps redact/expunge/black out the information about the things people start doing when affected by the colour and instead have this information in classified reports/logs. So people read about incidents that happened and get the information from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice format screw, but with enough content to stand on its own without it. Easy +1, nice job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As others have said, I really like that it can't make you do anything. It's just creepy and annoying, like a particularly immature 8-year-old who wants people to do gross stuff for its entertainment. Containment, therefore, is focused on "make that weirdo shut up already."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a synesthete who is, too, easily distracted by red, 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, it's "red text or a red background."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"red text on a red background"

Uh-oh, this is red text on a WHITE background! We're all doomed to hear a crazy megalomaniac voice in our brains forever! NOOOO!

(I did upvote, though, because this is still cool. Just nitpicking, lol.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was was building up to a great big deep sigh, until I read that the voice doesn't actually control people's minds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and compliments me on my bloodlust. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Nice and short, and decently original. I'm also a sucker for any SCPs relating to minor or ancient deities.

I expected it to also make you perform the acts it tells you to, simply for no reason but to make the SCP sound more dangerous and serious. I was happy when it didn't. Not every SCP has to make you die or go crazy to be entertaining. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goofy format screw, yes, but it works for me. It especially works because of how easily the entity is distracted, and the manner in which it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I strongly agree with your conclusion. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it in the draft stage, and I love it now. Peaceful (despite all the red), funny, entertaining, well-written… This is perfection in 213 words! Upvoted, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is somewhat on the wrong end of the gimmick-content balance for my tastes. I do appreciate that there is something there besides the format screw, which is not always the case around here.

What's there, however, is not grabbing me. Thematically, this seems to be a take on the association with the color red with angry/destructive/transgressive themes, but then you throw sex into it, and I have absolutely no idea what I'm supposed to take away from that. There's not really much to read into the entity other than "red=good" and thus not much to chew on, mentally speaking. That it's not a compulsion doesn't really affect my vote; avoiding actively bad writing isn't the same as good writing.

It's not a bad article. But it's not really moving the needle for me, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Attack of the ADHDemons!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably my favorite out of all the entries. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Give unto thine Lord blood sacrifices and shout unto — Oh, what's that? It's preeeeety……… Anyway, what was I saying?"

Basically what I was going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the direction you took this. It's not just deterred by the color red, it's actively distracted.

Like, "Give unto thine Lord blood sacrifices and shout unto — Oh, what's that? It's preeeeety……… Anyway, what was I saying?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm highly disappoint there was no hidden audio file to start playing while I read the article. Like, if you made one letter orange or something, near the end, that'd be great. As it is, I think there's enough here for an upvote, but it's just crying out for more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The brevity really did help this one in my opinion. Do not change that. I also really enjoyed the idea.

While the hallucinating a demon like voice, may seem overdone, I do like how you made it just simply a voice, and not a controlling power. That really helps separate it, though it does keep a small amount of "stereotypical antagonist" feel.

Also, is categorizing 2851 and 2851-1 really needed? It seems the voice is the anomaly, and not really an entity created by the anomaly. (Not sure on that one.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even better - urging, not compelling. Personally, if I heard an unseen voice telling me to steal, murder, and offer up sacrifices to some deity I had never even heard of, my impulse after a while would be to tell it to shut up, not to carry out its instructions. I got the feeling from this that the researchers were having the exact same response.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:

  • Interesting usage of the colour red.
  • It does have a meta feel, while not being the relatively less interesting sort and blends in with the internal logic of the SCP.

Cons:

  • A deity compelling others to do X, Y and Z is a tad bit old-fashioned for my tastes. And the deity's status is not really touched upon.

Verdict: Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you to everyone who reviewed and critiqued this. 213 words total.
